pattern: Neutralizing a cell is just an extra choice at every step, so tracking, alongside the usual grid-path best sum, how many of the up-to-two neutralizations have been spent turns this into ordinary right/down DP with one extra small dimension.
hints: This is the standard right-or-down grid DP, except each cell also carries a count of how many neutralizations have been used so far to reach it (0, 1, or 2).
Arriving at a cell, you either add its value as usual or spend one neutralization to add zero instead -- both options only ever look at the best value from directly above or directly to the left.

Compute the best total for every (cell, neutralizations-used) combination and take the best value at the destination cell across all three neutralization counts, since spending fewer than the full budget is always allowed.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, one robber worth neutralizing
statement example, all positive, no neutralization needed
more than two robbers on every path forces leaving one unneutralized
neutralizing is never forced, sometimes taking the loss is unavoidable

stress.description: moderate random grid with frequent negative robbers
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = max(1, min(size, 500))
    n = max(1, min(size, 500))
    grid = [[rng.randint(-1000, 1000) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [grid]
